content: Just as human-language lexical definition of reflexion as a metacognitive learning step in high school curricula frames deliberate reflection as a graded, teachable skill rather than an innate trait, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned as a scaffolded curriculum of incremental reflection exercises instead of a one-shot architectural module. This framework would train LLMs to build reflective competence step-by-step, mirroring how students master reflective writing over a unit of study.
